content: Multi-day itinerary splitting works via the TOPTW (Team Orienteering Problem with Time Windows) model. Key approach: supply algorithm with one 'trip' per day, each with start location, end location (hotel), and time budget (e.g., 08:00-22:00). Route optimizer assigns POIs to days based on time budget and geographic clustering. Practical implementation: first run nearest-neighbor+2-opt on all POIs to get optimized sequence, then greedily assign to days respecting daily time budget. If a POI doesn't fit in current day, push to next day.
